translated: Jacob had been with Laban for a long time and Jacob worked for Laban's daughters Leah and her sister Rachel and their wealth had increased greatly. But his love for Rachel was greater than all.

translated: Rachel also took her father's household gods and hid them in the camel's saddle and sat on them. Laban searched through everything in the tent but did not find them.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.

translated: Then Laban said to Jacob: "Because you have married Leah and Rachel, who are my daughters, and your children and their children are all my grandchildren and descendants, and your livestock are all mine. Everything you have belongs to me. But what can I do now for my daughters and the children they have borne?"

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”

translated: "The reason I am telling you this is so that you will have peace in me. In this world you will have trouble, but take courage because I have overcome the world."

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!

translated: And if you refuse to let my people go so that they may worship me, then tomorrow I will bring locusts into your territory, and they will cover the face of the land so that no one will be able to see the ground, and they will also eat what the Lord has left for you.

translated: And Joseph said to them: "Do not be afraid. Am I in the place of God? You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives."

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Josɛf ka ba fo mo daana na kutɔ nɛ b gbaŋ mbe piŋi tenteŋ muchɛr muchɛr nɛ e daŋ buu na
translated: Joseph came to his brothers and they stripped him of his beautiful colorful robe that he was wearing

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Ruben ka laŋɛ m ba ketirbu na ase m ba wu fanɛ Josɛf maŋ naa wɔtɔ nɛ e kpɛa mbe asɔbuuso to ŋkpal kagbenejijaso
translated: Reuben said to his father that they should kill him if he does not bring Joseph back and he gave his two sons as surety for his safe return

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Taama ka nu loŋ nɛ e delge mbe kukulwu be asɔbuuso nɛ e daŋ buu na n nase nseŋ ta asɔ pɔtɛ m buu n ta shata n deŋi kumu nɛ k baa buu mbe anishiakpa so. Kumo be kaman nɛ e ya chena kade nɛ baa tre Enayim na be ekarso. E daŋ wora loŋ nna ŋkpal e kaa nyi Juda pibinyɛn Shela ka daŋ ŋ ki enyɛn nɛ bumo alɛ daa maŋ shin nɛ e ya kil mo na so.
translated: Tamar took off her widow's clothes and covered herself with a veil, wrapping herself, and sat at the entrance to Enaim on the road to Timnah. For she saw that though Shelah had grown up, she had not been given to him as a wife.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Josɛf mo nyɛnpe Potifa ka wu fanɛ Enyɛnpe Ebɔrɛ na wɔ Josɛf kutɔ nsaa nɛfa kusɔ kama nɛ e bee wora na
translated: Joseph found favor in Potiphar's eyes and he became his personal servant, and God was with Joseph and gave him success in everything he did

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: nɛ Josɛf be asheŋ bɔlɔ mo kenishi ga. Ndoŋ nna nɛ e ta mo ŋ ki esa nɛ e bee shuŋ mbe ashuŋ nsaa keni mbe laŋ so.
translated: and Joseph was a very handsome young man. That is why his master's wife kept looking at him and wanted him to sleep with her.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Josɛf ka daŋ baa juŋkpar kusɔ kama ashi mbe laŋ to so, Potifa daŋ maŋ naa fɔŋ shɛŋ so ama ajibi nɛ e beeŋ ji nawule. Josɛf malɛ daŋ pɔr nɛnɛ nna a walɛ kayɛrbi.
translated: Joseph was doing all the work in the house very well, so Potiphar trusted him completely and put him in charge of everything. Joseph therefore became very successful in his master's household.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Kenimu be kaplɛa so, esa kama maŋ chɔ ma laŋ ere to. N nyɛnpe maa mɔn ma shɛŋ shɛŋ laŋ ere to ama fo nawule nɛ e maŋ ta m bɔɔ ma enɔ, ŋkpal manɛ so, mbe eche e la fo. To, nɛ nuso nɛ meeŋ tiŋ n nya kagbene kpakpaso n wora le be kulubi ŋ gbiti Ebɔrɛ?”
translated: If I had been a liar, I would not have told you this story. I would have kept quiet about it, but because of the love I have for you, that is why I am telling you this. So, what advice can you give me so that I can find a way to escape from God's punishment?

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Kachako nɛ Josɛf yɔ laŋ to nɛ e ya shuŋ nɛ laŋ na to be beshumpo na be ekama maŋ wɔtɔ.
translated: Kachako and Joseph went to the market and they bought things that the market people were selling at their stalls.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Kachako nɛ bumo basa anyɔ be ekama ku edare kanyɛ koŋwule na. Bumo be edareana na malɛ be kekama daa ŋini kusɔ ko nna.
translated: Cassava and yam are two things that are very important in our area. The two things are what we use to prepare our food.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Ama mee kule fo nna fanɛ nɛ kusɔ kama kaŋ nyalɛ n sa fo, fo nyiŋi ma nseŋ wora ma kelela. Kaŋɛ Fɛɛro ma asheŋ n lara ma ashi kabuti ere to.
translated: But if you refuse to let them go and still hold them, the hand of the Lord will bring a terrible plague on your livestock in the field. Then Pharaoh's heart became hard and he did not listen to them.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Edareana na be kaman be kachɛ sasopo na daa la Fɛɛro be kakurgeachɛ nna nɛ e wora kejigboŋ n sa mbe benimu kikɛ nseŋ lara asɔnuuso kenipo nimuso na nɛ bodobodo worapo nimuso na ashi kabuti to m ba benimu kikɛ be anishito.
translated: And they will no longer be plunder for the nations, nor will the wild animals of the land devour them, but they will live in safety, and no one will make them afraid, and I will provide for them a planting place of renown so that they will no longer be victims of famine in the land, and they will no longer bear the insults of the nations.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: anyi benyɔ na kikɛ daŋ ku edareana kanyɛ koŋwule nɛ amo be kekama daa kɔ kumo be kifito.
translated: and we should also know that the world's wealth and all its beauty will one day come to an end.

ref: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Fɛɛro kaŋɛ Josɛf le: “Ŋ ku edare nna nɛ ekama pin kumo be kifito ŋ gben. Ma alɛ nu b ka kaŋɛ fanɛ fee tiŋ a kute edare be afito.”
translated: Pharaoh said to Joseph: "I had a dream that no one could interpret for me. But I have heard it said about you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it."

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Josɛf kaŋɛ Fɛɛro le: “Fo edareana na be afito la kukoŋwule nna a maŋ kɔr abar to. Ewura Fɛɛro, Ebɔrɛ lara kusɔ nɛ e bee shin nɛ e wora nna ŋ ŋini fo.
translated: Then Joseph said to Pharaoh: "Your dreams are one and the same thing that will happen in the future. Lord Pharaoh, God has revealed the matter and has shown you what He will do."

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Ewura Fɛɛro, kusɔ nɛ k ba nɛ edare anyɔ na duli abar m ba fo kutɔ na e la fanɛ Ebɔrɛ yili kumo nna fanɛ abaanaaworasheŋ, e beeŋ wora kumo. K maaŋ chɛr gba.
translated: Lord God, thank you that you came and showed your love to the world and you gave your life for us so that we may have eternal life, and you are alive today. We praise your name.

ref: Your Majesty, you should find someone who is wise and will know what to do, so that you can put him in charge of all Egypt.
text: To, amoso, naniere a daga fanɛ fo fin esa nɛ e nyi asheŋ nsaa tiŋ a wora kusɔ nɛ k daga saŋkikɛ n shin nɛ e baa keni Ijipt be efuli ere be asheŋ so.
translated: So, today, if you look at it carefully, you will see that the same thing that happened in the past and caused suffering and made them go to Egypt, the same thing is happening again.

ref: The king told Joseph, “God is the one who has shown you these things. No one else is as wise as you are or knows as much as you do.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ Fɛɛro kaŋɛ Josɛf: “Ebɔrɛ ka baŋ lara le be asheŋ ere kikɛ ŋ ŋini fo ere, esa kama maŋ naa wɔtɔ a pin asheŋ to nsaa nyi asheŋ fanɛ fo.”
translated: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: "Because God has revealed all these things to you, there is no one as wise and understanding as you."

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Ade kikɛ be kaman nɛ Fɛɛro sa Josɛf ketre Zafenat Panea nseŋ ta On be kegbirwura Potifera pibiche nɛ baa tre Asenat na n sa mo fanɛ e baa la mbe eche. Josɛf ka nya loŋ be eyilikpa na nɛ e fara a nite Ijipt be efuli na kikɛ so.
translated: And Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-Paneah and gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, to be his wife. And Joseph went throughout the land of Egypt.

ref: I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down and buy some, so we won't starve to death.”
text: N nu fanɛ ajibi wɔ Ijipt, amoso men yɔ ndoŋ n ya tɔ ako m ba, saŋɛ na so akoŋ maaŋ mɔ anyi.”
translated: I will do wonders in Egypt, and after that he will let you go with your children.

ref: and I'm going to find out the truth. I swear by the life of the king that you won't leave this place until your youngest brother comes here.
text: Kananɛ anyi alɛ beeŋ wora m pin fanɛ kashenteŋ nɛ menyee ji nde: M bɔ ewura Fɛɛro be kuwurputi nɛ e tase so ere fanɛ men maaŋ lar nfe ama men be kaluge na baŋ ba nfe nna pɔɛŋ.
translated: But now they have seen these miracles and yet they hate both me and my Father, saying: I will remove Pharaoh's crown from his head so that I may become king, but they did not know that it would not happen that way.

ref: Choose one of you to go after your brother, while the rest of you stay here in jail. That will show whether you are telling the truth. But if you are lying, I swear by the life of the king that you are spies!
text: Amoso men shuŋi menyi to be eko nɛ e ya bar men sipobi na, ama baaŋ ti menyi nɛ men ka na ebu, saŋɛ na so anyeeŋ bɔla men gbagba be mmalga so ŋ keni, kashenteŋ nɛ menyee ji ere a? Nɛ k kaŋ ba fanɛ mannɛ kashenteŋ nɛ menyee ji, kumo ere m bɔ ewura Fɛɛro be kuwurputi fanɛ men ba nna nɛ men ba dara anyi be efuli nna.”
translated: Even though we know that it is not good to deceive you, but if we do not do it, or else these people will kill us all in war, what shall we do? And if we tell them what we know, then I will go to Pharaoh's palace and tell them that they have come to spy on the land.

ref: before saying to them: Since I respect God, I'll give you a chance to save your lives.
text: Kumo be kachɛ sasopo nɛ Josɛf kaŋɛ bumo le: “Mee ŋana Ebɔrɛ nna, amoso men wora kesheŋ nɛ mee shin nɛ ŋ kaŋɛ menyi ere nseŋ nya men be amu.
translated: The chief said to Joseph's brothers: "I know that you are Hebrews, but I want to test you to see if what you are saying is true."

ref: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
text: B daa maŋ nyi fanɛ Josɛf daa nu bumo be mmalga na, ŋkpal manɛ so, esa ko e daa sɔ mo so a kilgi mmalga na a sa bumo.
translated: They did not know that Joseph understood them because he spoke to them through an interpreter.

ref: Reuben spoke up, “Father, if I don't bring Benjamin back, you can kill both of my sons. Trust me with him, and I'll bring him back.”
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ruben kaŋɛ mo tuto le: “Ta Bɛnjamin m bɔɔ ma enɔ, meeŋ ya bɛta mo m ba. Nɛ m maŋ ya bɛta mo m bar fo, fo mɔ ma bibinyɛn anyɔ.”
translated: "Then Reuben said to his father: "If I do not bring Benjamin back to you, you may kill my two sons. Put him in my care and I will bring him back to you."

ref: They answered, “He asked a lot of questions about us and our family. He wanted to know if you were still alive and if we had any more brothers. All we could do was answer his questions. How could we know he would tell us to bring along our brother?”
text: Nɛ b kaŋɛ mo: “Kanyɛn na gbagba e lege anyi to m bishi anyi, anyi gbagba nɛ anyi be kanaŋ to ebi be asheŋ. E yɛ: ‘Men tuto kraa wɔtɔ a? Men kraa kɔ esipo nyɛnso a?’ Amoso mo ere be asheŋ bishiso na be afito nɛ anyi ere daŋ baa sa. Kumo be kaman nuso nɛ an daa wora a pin fanɛ e beeŋ kaŋɛ fanɛ: ‘Men naa ba, men keta men sipo na a ba?’ ”
translated: And they say: "Why should we tire ourselves out working for them when they tire themselves out with their worries. They say: 'What shall I eat? What shall I wear?' But those who worry like this and those who work hard, we all end up in the same place. So it is better that we don't work at all but just go around saying: 'What will come will come, whatever will be will be?'"

ref: Then Judah said to his father, “Let Benjamin go with me, and we will leave at once, so that none of us will starve to death.
text: Kamalga ere be kaman nɛ Juda kaŋɛ mo tuto Jeekɔb: “Ta kebia na m bɔɔ na enɔ nɛ an yɔ epul ere to, saŋɛ na so anyi nɛ fo nɛ anyi be mbia beeŋ baa wɔtɔ ŋkaa maaŋ wu.
translated: Kamarga and his brothers came to Judah's house and said to Jacob: "If you give me what I asked for and you refuse, then I will not go with you and I will not let my children go anywhere with you."

ref: Take Benjamin with you and leave at once.
text: Men ta men sipo gba n ti so n yɔ naniere.
translated: But we should not forget our culture.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ e chɔrɔ bumo nseŋ bishi bumo kananɛ kebaawɔtɔ du n sa bumo nɛ kananɛ bumo tuto du. E yɛ: “Men tuto kraa wɔtɔ a?”
translated: "The person who has two coats should share with the one who has none, and the one who has food should do the same. They asked: "Teacher, what should we do?"

ref: Later, Joseph told the servant in charge of his house, “Fill the men's grain sacks with as much as they can hold and put their money in the sacks. Also put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest brother.” The servant did as he was told.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ fo ta ma gbiti be kusɔ nuunchu na n ti mbe ayu be amansherbi so n wɔtɔ bumo be kaluge na be bɔtɛ to.” Ndoŋ nna nɛ kayɛrbi nimuso na wora asɔ nɛ Josɛf kaŋɛ fanɛ e wora na kikɛ.
translated: They should collect all the food of these good years that are coming and store up the grain under the authority of Pharaoh, to be kept in the cities for food." This is how the matter appeared good to Pharaoh and to all his servants, and Joseph was found to be wise.

ref: Early the next morning, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
text: Kumo be ŋklade, kaboŋ ka bee kpaŋɛ to nɛ Josɛf mo daana nɛ bumo be ekurma pɛ epe be ekpa.
translated: The people were happy, because they saw that Joseph's dream and what the people's chief said were the same.

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: B ka lar kade na to b daa maŋ naŋ fo kufɔ nɛ Josɛf kaŋɛ mbe kayɛrbi nimuso le: “Ju m bɛ basa na so epul ere to. Nɛ fo ya tu bumo fo bishi bumo le: ‘Manɛ e ba nɛ menyee ta kulubi a ka kelela be kukɔ?
translated: They said to each other and to their father about Joseph and his brothers' situation: "We are truly being punished for what we did to our brother. We saw his distress when he pleaded with us, but we would not listen."

ref: When the servant caught up with them, he said exactly what Joseph had told him to say.
text: Kayɛrbi na ka ya tu bumo nɛ e ta asheŋ nɛ mo nyɛnpe kaŋɛ na kikɛ chap ŋ kaŋɛ bumo.
translated: The elders should go and tell the chief that we want him to help us with something.

ref: Each of the brothers quickly put his sack on the ground and opened it.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ bumo be ekama nya manaŋ n suge mbe bɔtɛ n yili m bugi kumo so.
translated: That is why I want to thank all of you who helped me to reach my goal today.

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Nɛ Josɛf be kayɛrbi na fara a keni bumo be asɔ na to. Bumo kikɛ be enimuso peya to nɛ e fara ŋ keni kebɛso, kebɛso loŋ n ya fo bumo kikɛ to be kebia peya. E keni to loŋ n ya wu gbiti be kusɔnuunchu na Bɛnjamin be bɔtɛ to.
translated: And Joseph commanded his steward to fill their sacks with grain. The sacks were filled to the brim and he put their money back, the same money that was used to buy the grain was put back in their sacks. He also put his silver cup in Benjamin's sack.

ref: You asked us if our father was still alive and if we had any more brothers.
text: Fo, n nyɛnpe e daŋ bishi anyi fo anya le: ‘Men kɔ etuto ŋko esipobi a?’
translated: So, if someone does something bad to you, you should not say: 'Why did this happen to me?'

ref: But we told him, “We can't go back to Egypt without our youngest brother. We will never be let in to see the governor, unless he is with us.”
text: Nɛ an kaŋɛ mo: ‘Ayai, an sipobi ere baa maŋ tu anyi n yɔ, anyi maaŋ tiŋ n yɔ, ŋkpal manɛ so, an maaŋ ya tiŋ n wu kanyɛn na, ama an sipobi ere baa wɔtɔ nna.’
translated: And they said to him: 'Father, your younger children who are here with us, we are staying with them, we are living with them, but now, we are not staying with them in peace, because your younger children are troubling us.'

ref: That's why Benjamin must be with us when I go back to my father. He loves him so much
text: Amoso nɛ m baŋ bɛta n yɔ n tuto, fo kenya na kutɔ ŋkaa maŋ keta kebia na n yɔ
translated: Today when I want to speak my language, you say that the thing I am doing is not good

ref: Since Joseph could no longer control his feelings in front of his servants, he sent them out of the room. When he was alone with his brothers, he told them, “I am Joseph.”
text: K ka fo le na, Josɛf daa maŋ naŋ tiŋ nɛ e keta mbe kumu nseŋ kaŋɛ basa pɔtɛ nɛ b daa wɔ ndoŋ na le: “Ekama e lar nfe ŋ ka anyi.” Amoso esa pɔtɛ kama daa maŋ naa wɔ Josɛf kutɔ saŋɛ nɛ e lara mbe kumu ŋ ŋini mo daana
translated: And it happened that Joseph was there with them in prison, and the chief cupbearer and the chief baker who were imprisoned said: "Each of us had a dream last night." But there was no one to interpret Joseph's dreams, so he said to them, "Tell me your dreams."

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: nseŋ shu awɔrso ga hale nɛ Ijiptebi nɛ b daa wɔ kowu na nu e ka bee shu. Ewura Fɛɛro be laŋ to ebi gba nu mbe kushu na be asheŋ.
translated: The Israelites cried out to the Lord because of the Egyptians who were treating them harshly. The Lord heard their cry and remembered his covenant with their ancestors.

ref: But God sent me on ahead of you to keep your families alive and to save you in this wonderful way.
text: Ama Ebɔrɛ shin nna nɛ n juŋkpar menyi m ba nfe nɛ m ba bɔla le be ekpa ere so m mɔlga menyi, saŋɛ na so, menyi nɛ men be kanaŋ to ebi maaŋ mur.
translated: But God has shown me that I should not call any person common or unclean, so I came, when I was called without objection.

ref: I will take care of you there during the next five years of famine. But if you don't come, you and your family and your animals will starve to death.”
text: Fo ba kaa wɔ Goshen, meeŋ baa keni fo nɛ fo kanaŋ to ebi so, ŋkpal manɛ so, nfɛ anu mo nɛ a kraa wɔ anyi be anishito ere kraa la akoŋ be nfɛ nna. Ma alɛ maa sha fanɛ akoŋ nɛ ketentaŋase e mɔ fo nɛ fo kowu to ebi nɛ fo basa kikɛ nɛ fo asɔbɔya kikɛ.’ ”
translated: When you arrive in Goshen, and your father and your brothers come to you, and Pharaoh speaks to you and says, 'What is your occupation?' you shall say, 'Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,' in order that you may dwell in the land of Goshen, for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.

ref: Tell my father about my great power here in Egypt and about everything you have seen. Hurry and bring him here.
text: Men kaŋɛ n tuto bunyaŋ nɛ b kɔ a sa ma Ijipt nfe nseŋ kaŋɛ mo kusɔ kama nyam nɛ men wu nfe ere be asheŋ nseŋ bar n tuto nfe manaŋ.”
translated: And now I have heard the murmuring which the children of Israel murmur against me, saying that in Egypt we sat by the meat pots and we ate bread until we were satisfied.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Josɛf mo daana na nɛ mo sipo Bɛnjamin be keba na be baru ka fo ewura Fɛɛro pe nɛ mo nɛ mbe benimu kikɛ be ŋgbene fuli bumo ga.
translated: Joseph took his father and his younger brother Benjamin and they went and stood before Pharaoh and he and his brothers told him about their work.

ref: They can leave their possessions behind, because they will be given the best of everything in Egypt.
text: Men sa maŋ ya shin nɛ men be asɔ nɛ men maaŋ teŋ bar na be asheŋ e tir menyi, ŋkpal manɛ so, menyeeŋ nya asɔ lela Ijipt a chɔ amo.’ ”
translated: "If we find favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as our possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan.'"
